tEn: DeepSeek-Prover: Advancing Theorem Proving in LLMs through Large-Scale Synthetic Data
tZh: DeepSeek-Prover：通过大规模合成数据推进大语言模型中的定理证明
sEn: Large-scale synthetic data from math competition problems boosts LLM theorem proving, with DeepSeekMath 7B achieving 46.3% accuracy on miniF2F, surpassing GPT-4.
sZh: 利用数学竞赛问题生成的大规模合成数据显著提升了大语言模型的定理证明能力，DeepSeekMath 7B在miniF2F测试中达到46.3%的准确率，超越了GPT-4。
absEn: Proof assistants like Lean have revolutionized mathematical proof verification, ensuring high accuracy and reliability. Although large language models (LLMs) show promise in mathematical reasoning, their advancement in formal theorem proving is hindered by a lack of training data. To address this issue, we introduce an approach to generate extensive Lean 4 proof data derived from high-school and undergraduate-level mathematical competition problems. This approach involves translating natural language problems into formal statements, filtering out low-quality statements, and generating proofs to create synthetic data. After fine-tuning the DeepSeekMath 7B model on this synthetic dataset, which comprises 8 million formal statements with proofs, our model achieved whole-proof generation accuracies of 46.3% with 64 samples and 52% cumulatively on the Lean 4 miniF2F test, surpassing the baseline GPT-4 at 23.0% with 64 samples and a tree search reinforcement learning method at 41.0%. Additionally, our model successfully proved 5 out of 148 problems in the Lean 4 Formalized International Mathematical Olympiad (FIMO) benchmark, while GPT-4 failed to prove any. These results demonstrate the potential of leveraging large-scale synthetic data to enhance theorem-proving capabilities in LLMs. Both the synthetic dataset and the model will be made available to facilitate further research in this promising field.
absZh: 像Lean这样的证明助手彻底改变了数学证明的验证方式，确保了高准确性和可靠性。尽管大语言模型在数学推理方面显示出潜力，但它们在形式定理证明方面的进展受到训练数据缺乏的阻碍。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种方法，从高中和本科水平的数学竞赛问题中生成大量的Lean 4证明数据。该方法包括将自然语言问题转化为形式化陈述，过滤低质量陈述，并生成证明以创建合成数据。在包含800万条形式化陈述及其证明的合成数据集上微调DeepSeekMath 7B模型后，我们的模型在Lean 4 miniF2F测试中实现了46.3%（64个样本）的整句证明生成准确率，累计准确率达到52%，超过了基线GPT-4（64个样本准确率23.0%）和树搜索强化学习方法（准确率41.0%）。此外，我们的模型在Lean 4形式化国际数学奥林匹克（FIMO）基准测试中成功证明了148个问题中的5个，而GPT-4未能证明任何问题。这些结果表明，利用大规模合成数据来增强大语言模型的定理证明能力具有巨大潜力。合成数据集和模型都将公开，以促进这一有前景领域的进一步研究。

contrib.en: Proposes an iterative method to synthesize 8 million formal statements with proofs from informal math problems.
contrib.zh: 提出一种迭代方法，从非正式数学问题中合成800万条带证明的形式化语句。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art whole-proof generation accuracy of 46.3% on miniF2F with 64 samples, surpassing GPT-4 and RL methods.
contrib.zh: 在miniF2F上以64个样本实现46.3%的整句证明生成准确率，超越GPT-4和强化学习方法。

contrib.en: Successfully proves 5 out of 148 FIMO problems, while GPT-4 proves none.
contrib.zh: 成功证明FIMO基准中148个问题中的5个，而GPT-4未能证明任何问题。

contrib.en: Introduces dual concurrent proof search for statements and their negations to improve efficiency.
contrib.zh: 引入对语句及其否定进行双重并发证明搜索，以提高效率。

contrib.en: Demonstrates clear correlation between synthetic dataset size and model performance on theorem proving.
contrib.zh: 证明合成数据集规模与定理证明模型性能之间存在明确相关性。

contrib.en: Open-sources the large-scale synthetic dataset and model to facilitate further research.
contrib.zh: 开源大规模合成数据集和模型，以促进进一步研究。

limits.en: Focuses primarily on algebra and number theory at high school and undergraduate levels.
limits.zh: 主要关注高中和本科水平的代数和数论问题。

limits.en: Synthetic data quality depends on the base model's autoformalization capability.
limits.zh: 合成数据质量依赖于基础模型的自动形式化能力。

limits.en: Proof search may still be computationally intensive despite dual concurrent approach.
limits.zh: 尽管采用双重并发方法，证明搜索仍可能计算密集。

limits.en: Generalizability to advanced mathematical topics requiring complex definitions is limited.
limits.zh: 对需要复杂定义的高级数学主题的泛化能力有限。

limits.en: Performance on benchmarks may not fully reflect real-world theorem proving scenarios.
limits.zh: 在基准测试上的表现可能无法完全反映真实世界的定理证明场景。

items.en: Proof assistants like Lean have revolutionized mathematical proof verification, ensuring high accuracy and reliability. Although large language models (LLMs) show promise in mathematical reasoning, their advancement in formal theorem proving is hindered by a lack of training data. To address this issue, we introduce an approach to generate extensive Lean 4 proof data derived from high-school and undergraduate-level mathematical competition problems. This approach involves translating natural language problems into formal statements, filtering out low-quality statements, and generating proofs to create synthetic data. After fine-tuning the DeepSeekMath 7B model on this synthetic dataset, which comprises 8 million formal statements with proofs, our model achieved whole-proof generation accuracies of 46.3% with 64 samples and 52% cumulatively on the Lean 4 miniF2F test, surpassing the baseline GPT-4 at 23.0% with 64 samples and a tree search reinforcement learning method at 41.0%. Additionally, our model successfully proved 5 out of 148 problems in the Lean 4 Formalized International Mathematical Olympiad (FIMO) benchmark, while GPT-4 failed to prove any. These results demonstrate the potential of leveraging large-scale synthetic data to enhance theorem-proving capabilities in LLMs. Both the synthetic dataset and the model will be made available to facilitate further research in this promising field.
items.zh: 像 Lean 这样的证明助手彻底改变了数学证明验证，确保了高准确性和可靠性。尽管大型语言模型（LLM）在数学推理方面显示出潜力，但它们在形式定理证明中的进展因缺乏训练数据而受阻。为解决这一问题，我们引入了一种方法，从高中和本科水平的数学竞赛问题中生成大量的 Lean 4 证明数据。该方法包括将自然语言问题翻译成形式化陈述，过滤低质量陈述，并生成证明以创建合成数据。在包含 800 万个带证明的形式化陈述的合成数据集上微调 DeepSeekMath 7B 模型后，我们的模型在 Lean 4 miniF2F 测试中实现了 46.3%（64 个样本）和 52%（累积）的完整证明生成准确率，超过了基线 GPT-4（64 个样本为 23.0%）和树搜索强化学习方法（41.0%）。此外，我们的模型在 Lean 4 形式化国际数学奥林匹克（FIMO）基准测试中成功证明了 148 个问题中的 5 个，而 GPT-4 未能证明任何问题。这些结果展示了利用大规模合成数据增强 LLM 定理证明能力的潜力。合成数据集和模型将公开提供，以促进这一有前景领域的进一步研究。

items.en: In modern mathematics, the increasing complexity of proofs presents substantial challenges for peer review. This complexity has led to the acceptance of erroneous proofs, with critical flaws often detected only after considerable time. To address these issues, formal mathematical languages such as Lean (De Moura et al., 2015; Moura and Ullrich, 2021), Isabelle (Paulson, 1994), and Coq (The Coq Development Team, ) have been developed. These languages enable the creation of computer-verifiable proofs (Avigad, 2023). However, crafting formal proofs demands significant effort, specialized expertise, and poses challenges even for seasoned mathematicians. Consequently, the significance of automated theorem proving is on the rise (Shulman, 2024).
items.zh: 在现代数学中，证明的日益复杂给同行评审带来了巨大挑战。这种复杂性导致错误证明被接受，关键缺陷往往在相当长的时间后才被发现。为解决这些问题，形式化数学语言如 Lean（De Moura 等人，2015；Moura 和 Ullrich，2021）、Isabelle（Paulson，1994）和 Coq（Coq 开发团队）被开发出来。这些语言使得创建计算机可验证的证明成为可能（Avigad，2023）。然而，编写形式化证明需要大量努力、专业知识，甚至对经验丰富的数学家来说也充满挑战。因此，自动定理证明的重要性日益上升（Shulman，2024）。

items.en: To reduce the effort involved in writing formal mathematical proofs, several approaches (Polu and Sutskever, 2020; Jiang et al., 2021; Han et al., 2021; Polu et al., 2022; Lample et al., 2022; Jiang et al., 2022a; Yang et al., 2024) have been developed, primarily focusing on search algorithms that explore potential solutions for proposed theorems. However, these methods often struggle with the vast search spaces required for complex theorems, rendering them ineffective for more intricate proofs (Loos et al., 2017). Recently, advances in large language models (LLMs) have introduced a novel strategy, utilizing pre-trained models to guide the search process. Although these new methods (Jiang et al., 2022b; Zhao et al., 2023; Xin et al., 2023) represent significant improvements, they still fall short of practical applicability due to the lack of parallel corpus. Unlike conventional programming languages such as Python or Java, formal proof languages are used by relatively few mathematicians, resulting in limited datasets. Recent advances in autoformalization (Wu et al., 2022) allow more aligned data to be synthesized to train LLM-based automated theorem provers. Nevertheless, the resulting dataset remains too small to fully unleash the capabilities of LLMs.
items.zh: 为减少编写形式化数学证明的工作量，已开发出多种方法（Polu 和 Sutskever，2020；Jiang 等人，2021；Han 等人，2021；Polu 等人，2022；Lample 等人，2022；Jiang 等人，2022a；Yang 等人，2024），主要集中于探索所提出定理潜在解的搜索算法。然而，这些方法通常难以应对复杂定理所需的大规模搜索空间，使其对更复杂的证明无效（Loos 等人，2017）。最近，大型语言模型（LLM）的进步引入了一种新策略，利用预训练模型引导搜索过程。尽管这些新方法（Jiang 等人，2022b；Zhao 等人，2023；Xin 等人，2023）代表了显著改进，但由于缺乏平行语料库，它们仍达不到实际应用。与 Python 或 Java 等传统编程语言不同，形式化证明语言仅被相对较少的数学家使用，导致数据集有限。自动形式化（Wu 等人，2022）的最新进展允许合成更多对齐的数据来训练基于 LLM 的自动定理证明器。然而，生成的数据集仍然太小，无法充分发挥 LLM 的能力。

items.en: To address this issue, we propose a method for generating extensive Lean 4 proof data from informal mathematical problems. Our approach translates high-school and undergraduate-level mathematical competition problems into formal statements. We then automate proof generation using a large language model (LLM) and verify the correctness of these proofs within the Lean 4 environment. The primary challenge of this method is to ensure both the scale and quality of the synthetic data.
items.zh: 为解决这一问题，我们提出了一种从非正式数学问题生成大量 Lean 4 证明数据的方法。我们的方法将高中和本科水平的数学竞赛问题翻译成形式化陈述。然后，我们使用大型语言模型（LLM）自动化证明生成，并在 Lean 4 环境中验证这些证明的正确性。该方法的主要挑战是确保合成数据的规模和质量。

items.en: Quality Assurance: We enhance the quality of generated proofs through a multi-step process. First, we filter out simple statements using a quality scoring model and exclude invalid statements via a hypothesis rejection strategy. Our novel iterative framework then improves proof quality by initially generating synthetic statements from informal math problems using an under-trained LLM fine-tuned on limited data. These statements are used to generate corresponding proofs, which are validated for correctness using a Lean 4 verifier. The correct theorem-proof pairs are subsequently used to further train the initial model. Through several iterations, the model trained on large-scale synthetic data becomes significantly more powerful than the originally under-trained LLMs, resulting in higher-quality theorem-proof pairs.
items.zh: 质量保证：我们通过多步骤流程提高生成证明的质量。首先，我们使用质量评分模型过滤简单陈述，并通过假设拒绝策略排除无效陈述。然后，我们新颖的迭代框架通过以下方式提高证明质量：最初使用在有限数据上微调的欠训练 LLM 从非正式数学问题生成合成陈述。这些陈述用于生成相应的证明，并使用 Lean 4 验证器验证其正确性。正确的定理-证明对随后用于进一步训练初始模型。经过几次迭代，在大规模合成数据上训练的模型变得比最初欠训练的 LLM 强大得多，从而产生更高质量的定理-证明对。

items.en: Scale Assurance: To accelerate the proof generation process, our method addresses the challenge of the large search space for proofs. A significant cause of delays is the generation of unprovable statements that continue to be processed until they reach the time limit. To mitigate this, we propose proving negated statements in parallel. Once either the original statement or its negation is proved, the entire proving process is terminated.
items.zh: 规模保证：为加速证明生成过程，我们的方法解决了证明搜索空间大的挑战。延迟的一个重要原因是生成了不可证明的陈述，这些陈述会一直处理直到达到时间限制。为缓解这一问题，我们提出并行证明否定陈述。一旦原始陈述或其否定被证明，整个证明过程即终止。

items.en: We assess the effectiveness of our method on Lean 4 theorem proving using 488 problems from miniF2F (Zheng et al., 2021) and 148 problems from the FIMO benchmarks (Liu et al., 2023). We utilize DeepSeekMath 7B (Shao et al., 2024), a state-of-the-art mathematical model, as our base. The results show that our iteratively trained model performs strongly, achieving 46.3% accuracy in whole-proof generation on the miniF2F-test benchmark with 64 samples, surpassing GPT-4 (Achiam et al., 2023) at 23.0% and a reinforcement learning method at 41.0%. Additionally, our approach solved 4 out of 148 problems in the FIMO benchmark with 100 samples, while GPT-4 solved none, and our approach solved 5 with 4096 samples. Ablation experiments indicate that the model progressively solves more problems in miniF2F with each iteration. In summary, our paper makes the following contributions:
items.zh: 我们使用 miniF2F（Zheng 等人，2021）中的 488 个问题和 FIMO 基准（Liu 等人，2023）中的 148 个问题评估了我们的方法在 Lean 4 定理证明上的有效性。我们使用 DeepSeekMath 7B（Shao 等人，2024），一个最先进的数学模型，作为基础。结果表明，我们迭代训练的模型表现强劲，在 miniF2F-test 基准上使用 64 个样本实现了 46.3% 的完整证明生成准确率，超过了 GPT-4（Achiam 等人，2023）的 23.0% 和强化学习方法的 41.0%。此外，我们的方法在 FIMO 基准上使用 100 个样本解决了 148 个问题中的 4 个，而 GPT-4 未解决任何问题，使用 4096 个样本解决了 5 个。消融实验表明，模型在每次迭代中逐步解决了 miniF2F 中的更多问题。总之，我们的论文做出了以下贡献：

items.en: We introduce an iterative method to synthesize 8 million formal statements, each accompanied by a formal proof, from informal math problems. Experimental results demonstrate that this method significantly enhances both the scalability and quality of synthetic data.
items.zh: 我们引入了一种迭代方法，从非正式数学问题中合成了 800 万个形式化陈述，每个陈述都附有形式化证明。实验结果表明，该方法显著提高了合成数据的可扩展性和质量。

items.en: Our model, trained on this synthetic dataset, achieves state-of-the-art performance on benchmarks, with whole-proof generation accuracies of 46.3% using 64 samples and 52% cumulatively on the Lean 4 miniF2F test. This surpasses the baseline GPT-4 at 23.0% with 64 samples and a tree search reinforcement learning method at 41.0%. Additionally, our model successfully proved 5 out of 148 problems in the Lean 4 Formalized International Mathematical Olympiad (FIMO) benchmark, while GPT-4 failed to prove any.
items.zh: 我们的模型在该合成数据集上训练，在基准测试中达到了最先进的性能，在 Lean 4 miniF2F 测试中使用 64 个样本实现了 46.3% 的完整证明生成准确率，累积准确率为 52%。这超过了基线 GPT-4（64 个样本为 23.0%）和树搜索强化学习方法（41.0%）。此外，我们的模型在 Lean 4 形式化国际数学奥林匹克（FIMO）基准测试中成功证明了 148 个问题中的 5 个，而 GPT-4 未能证明任何问题。

items.en: We contribute to the mathematical and AI communities by creating and open-sourcing a large dataset of high-quality formal mathematical proofs, thereby fostering further research and development in automated theorem proving.
items.zh: 我们通过创建并开源一个大型高质量形式化数学证明数据集，为数学和人工智能社区做出贡献，从而促进自动定理证明的进一步研究和发展。

items.en: Automated theorem proving has been a significant area of interest in artificial intelligence research since its inception (Bibel, 2013). Initial efforts were directed at simpler logical frameworks, which led to the development of highly efficient first-order theorem provers like E (Schulz, 2002) and Vampire (Kovács and Voronkov, 2013). Nonetheless, these tools often fall short in handling complex theorems commonly found in modern proof assistants such as Lean (De Moura et al., 2015), Isabelle (Paulson, 1994), and Coq (The Coq Development Team, ). The advent of recent deep learning models and model-guided search techniques has reinvigorated the field (Bansal et al., 2019). This modern approach has not only enhanced the capabilities of ATP systems but also expanded their applicability in solving more intricate mathematical problems.
items.zh: 自动定理证明自人工智能研究诞生以来一直是一个重要领域（Bibel，2013）。最初的努力针对更简单的逻辑框架，导致了像 E（Schulz，2002）和 Vampire（Kovács 和 Voronkov，2013）这样的高效一阶定理证明器的发展。然而，这些工具在处理现代证明助手（如 Lean（De Moura 等人，2015）、Isabelle（Paulson，1994）和 Coq（Coq 开发团队））中常见的复杂定理时往往力不从心。近期深度学习模型和模型引导搜索技术的出现重新激发了该领域（Bansal 等人，2019）。这种现代方法不仅增强了 ATP 系统的能力，还扩展了其在解决更复杂数学问题中的适用性。

items.en: ATP with Neural Models. With the development of deep learning, several approaches have been proposed to combine neural models with ATP (Loos et al., 2017). A series of ATP approaches adopts tree search algorithms guided by neural models (Polu and Sutskever, 2020; Han et al., 2021; Polu et al., 2022; Jiang et al., 2022a; Yang et al., 2024). These approaches primarily utilize reinforcement learning techniques to enhance the accuracy of the model (Kaliszyk et al., 2018; Crouse et al., 2021; Wu et al., 2021; Lample et al., 2022). Since the search space is significantly large, the searching process consumes considerable time and computing resources.
items.zh: 带神经模型的 ATP。随着深度学习的发展，已提出多种将神经模型与 ATP 结合的方法（Loos 等人，2017）。一系列 ATP 方法采用由神经模型引导的树搜索算法（Polu 和 Sutskever，2020；Han 等人，2021；Polu 等人，2022；Jiang 等人，2022a；Yang 等人，2024）。这些方法主要利用强化学习技术来提高模型的准确性（Kaliszyk 等人，2018；Crouse 等人，2021；Wu 等人，2021；Lample 等人，2022）。由于搜索空间非常大，搜索过程消耗大量时间和计算资源。

items.en: Another series of ATP approaches harnesses the power of large language models. These approaches typically involve language models that are fine-tuned with open-source proof data and interact with verifiers via a state-action transition program (Polu and Sutskever, 2020; Jiang et al., 2021; Han et al., 2021; Polu et al., 2022; Lample et al., 2022; Jiang et al., 2022a; Yang et al., 2024). This process iteratively generates proof steps and verifies their correctness with formal verifiers. It then generates the next proof steps based on the proof states returned by the formal verifiers. Although these approaches achieve high performance, they are computationally intensive. To enhance efficiency, recent researches leverage language models to generate complete formal proofs directly (First et al., 2023; Jiang et al., 2022b; Zhao et al., 2023; Xin et al., 2023), thus bypassing the iterative interaction during proof generation.
items.zh: 另一系列 ATP 方法利用大型语言模型的力量。这些方法通常涉及使用开源证明数据微调的语言模型，并通过状态-动作转换程序与验证器交互（Polu 和 Sutskever，2020；Jiang 等人，2021；Han 等人，2021；Polu 等人，2022；Lample 等人，2022；Jiang 等人，2022a；Yang 等人，2024）。该过程迭代生成证明步骤，并用形式化验证器验证其正确性。然后根据形式化验证器返回的证明状态生成下一步证明步骤。尽管这些方法实现了高性能，但计算密集。为提高效率，最近的研究利用语言模型直接生成完整的正式证明（First 等人，2023；Jiang 等人，2022b；Zhao 等人，2023；Xin 等人，2023），从而绕过了证明生成过程中的迭代交互。

items.en: Autoformalization for Formal Mathematics. Due to the limited availability of formal corpora for training, the performance of current large language models (LLMs) is also constrained. Thus, some approaches propose autoformalization (Wu et al., 2022; Jiang et al., 2022b), which involves converting natural language descriptions into formal statements that can be verified by proof assistants. Several studies have generated synthetic datasets of formal proofs using rule-based transformations of existing theorems (Wu et al., 2020; Wang and Deng, 2020; Xiong et al., 2023). While effective, these methods are constrained by their reliance on predefined rules and lack flexibility for broader applications. Recent methodologies adopt large language models to translating natural language problems into formal statements (Huang et al., 2024). However, these datasets remain smaller than needed and are limited to small mathematical benchmarks, leading to only minor improvements in training outcomes for language models. In this paper, we aim to synthesise formal proofs via autoformalization at a much larger scale to boost the performance of a neural prover.
items.zh: 形式化数学的自动形式化。由于用于训练的形式化语料库有限，当前大型语言模型（LLM）的性能也受到限制。因此，一些方法提出了自动形式化（Wu 等人，2022；Jiang 等人，2022b），即将自然语言描述转换为可由证明助手验证的形式化陈述。几项研究使用基于规则的现有定理转换生成了形式化证明的合成数据集（Wu 等人，2020；Wang 和 Deng，2020；Xiong 等人，2023）。虽然有效，但这些方法受限于对预定义规则的依赖，缺乏更广泛应用的灵活性。最近的方法采用大型语言模型将自然语言问题翻译成形式化陈述（Huang 等人，2024）。然而，这些数据集仍然比所需的小，并且仅限于小型数学基准，导致语言模型训练结果的改进微乎其微。在本文中，我们旨在通过自动形式化以更大规模合成形式化证明，以提升神经证明器的性能。

items.capEn: Figure 1: An overview of our approach.

items.en: In this section, we introduce our approach, which consists of four key processes as depicted in Figure 1. The initial phase concentrates on generating formal mathematical statements from a broad collection of informal math problems, necessitating further proof. Next, the autoformalized statements are filtered through model scoring and hypothesis rejection methods to select high-quality statements. These statements are then proved by a model called DeepSeek-Prover, with their correctness verified by the formal verifier called Lean 4111𝚕𝚎𝚊𝚗𝚙𝚛𝚘𝚟𝚎𝚛/𝚕𝚎𝚊𝚗𝟺:𝚟𝟺​.7.0−𝚛𝚌𝟸:𝚕𝚎𝚊𝚗𝚙𝚛𝚘𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚕𝚎𝚊𝚗𝟺𝚟𝟺.7.0𝚛𝚌𝟸\mathtt{leanprover/lean4:v4.7.0-rc2}, yielding validated formal statements and proofs. These data serve as synthetic data for fine-tuning the DeepSeek-Prover. After enhancing DeepSeek-Prover, we repeat the entire previously described process. This cycle continues until the improvements in DeepSeek-Prover become marginal. Notably, to enhance proof efficiency, we prove concurrently both the original statements and their negations. This method has the advantage of swiftly discarding the original statement when it is invalid by proving its negation. The details of each phase will be described in the subsequent sections.
items.zh: 在本节中，我们介绍我们的方法，该方法包括四个关键过程，如图 1 所示。初始阶段专注于从大量非正式数学问题生成形式化数学陈述，这些陈述需要进一步证明。接下来，通过模型评分和假设拒绝方法过滤自动形式化的陈述，以选择高质量陈述。然后，这些陈述由名为 DeepSeek-Prover 的模型证明，其正确性由名为 Lean 4 的形式化验证器验证，产生经过验证的形式化陈述和证明。这些数据作为合成数据用于微调 DeepSeek-Prover。在增强 DeepSeek-Prover 后，我们重复整个先前描述的过程。此循环持续直到 DeepSeek-Prover 的改进变得微不足道。值得注意的是，为提高证明效率，我们同时证明原始陈述及其否定。这种方法的好处是，当原始陈述无效时，通过证明其否定可以快速丢弃它。每个阶段的细节将在后续章节中描述。

items.en: The generation of formal proof data fundamentally relies on the availability of a substantial corpus of formal statements. In practice, however, amassing a large collection of manually crafted formal statements is challenging. Fortunately, the internet is replete with math-related problems expressed in natural language. By autoformalizing these informal mathematical problems, we can generate a vast repository of formal statements.
items.zh: 形式化证明数据的生成从根本上依赖于大量形式化陈述的可用性。然而，在实践中，收集大量手工制作的形式化陈述具有挑战性。幸运的是，互联网上充斥着用自然语言表达的数学相关问题。通过自动形式化这些非正式数学问题，我们可以生成一个庞大的形式化陈述库。

items.en: We have observed that problems with explicit conditions and well-defined goals are typically easier to formalize compared to advanced mathematical topics that necessitate intricate definitions and constructions. Consequently, this paper primarily examines high school and undergraduate-level competition problems, with a particular emphasis on algebra and number theory, and to a lesser extent, combinatorics, geometry, and statistics. Despite their apparent simplicity, these problems often involve complex solution techniques, making them excellent candidates for constructing proof data to improve theorem-proving capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs). To compile our dataset, we employed web scraping and careful data cleaning techniques to extract problems from online resources featuring high school and undergraduate exercises, exams, and competitions, resulting in a dataset of 869,659 high-quality natural language math problems.
items.zh: 我们观察到，与需要复杂定义和构造的高级数学主题相比，具有明确条件和明确定义目标的问题通常更容易形式化。因此，本文主要研究高中和本科水平的竞赛问题，特别侧重于代数和数论，以及较小程度的组合学、几何和统计学。尽管看似简单，这些问题通常涉及复杂的解题技巧，使其成为构建证明数据以提高大型语言模型（LLM）定理证明能力的优秀候选。为编译我们的数据集，我们采用了网络爬虫和仔细的数据清洗技术，从包含高中和本科练习、考试和竞赛的在线资源中提取问题，最终得到一个包含 869,659 个高质量自然语言数学问题的数据集。

items.en: Specifically, we initialized the DeepSeek-Prover using the DeepSeekMath-Base 7B model (Shao et al., 2024). Initially, the model struggled to convert informal math problems into formal statements. To address this, we fine-tuned the DeepSeek-Prover model using the MMA dataset (Jiang et al., 2023), which comprises formal statements from Lean 4’s mathlib222The specific mathlib commit used is 𝟼𝟺𝟻𝟸𝟾𝟸𝟼𝟾​𝚋​𝟹​𝚌​𝟸​𝚌​𝚏​𝟻𝟽𝟾𝟼𝟹𝟿​𝚋​𝚌​𝟺𝟽𝟿𝟾𝟸𝟾𝟾𝟾𝟸​𝚊​𝟿​𝚎​𝚌​𝚍​𝟹​𝚊​𝟾𝟸64528268𝚋3𝚌2𝚌𝚏578639𝚋𝚌479828882𝚊9𝚎𝚌𝚍3𝚊82\mathtt{64528268b3c2cf578639bc479828882a9ecd3a82}. that were back-translated into natural language problem descriptions by GPT-4. We then instructed the model to translate these natural language problems into formal statements in Lean 4 using a structured approach.
items.zh: 具体来说，我们使用 DeepSeekMath-Base 7B 模型（Shao 等人，2024）初始化了 DeepSeek-Prover。最初，该模型难以将非正式数学问题转换为形式化陈述。为解决这一问题，我们使用 MMA 数据集（Jiang 等人，2023）对 DeepSeek-Prover 模型进行了微调，该数据集包含来自 Lean 4 的 mathlib 的形式化陈述，这些陈述由 GPT-4 反向翻译为自然语言问题描述。然后，我们指导模型使用结构化方法将这些自然语言问题翻译成 Lean 4 中的形式化陈述。

items.en: Prompt: Mathematical Problem in Natural Language: {$informal_statement_with_answers} Translate the problem to Lean 4 (only the core declaration): ‘‘‘lean4
items.zh: 提示：自然语言中的数学问题：{$informal_statement_with_answers} 将问题翻译成 Lean 4（仅核心声明）：‘‘‘lean4

items.en: The quality of the autoformalized statements was found to be suboptimal due to two main issues. Firstly, many formal statements were overly simplistic. To address this, we developed scoring criteria and provided examples from miniF2F-valid as few-shot examples to guide the DeepSeek-Prover model in evaluating the content and quality of these statements using a chain-of-thought approach. Manual review of these scores confirmed that the model’s evaluations closely matched human intuition and expectations. Specifically, the model was instructed (see Appendix A.1 for the detailed prompt) to classify the quality of each formal statement into categories: "excellent," "good," "above average," "fair," or "poor." Statements rated as "fair" or "poor" were subsequently excluded.
items.zh: 由于两个主要问题，自动形式化语句的质量被发现不够理想。首先，许多形式化语句过于简单。为了解决这个问题，我们制定了评分标准，并提供了来自 miniF2F-valid 的示例作为少样本示例，以指导 DeepSeek-Prover 模型使用思维链方法评估这些语句的内容和质量。对这些分数的人工审查确认，模型的评估与人类的直觉和期望高度吻合。具体来说，模型被指示（详细提示见附录 A.1）将每个形式化语句的质量分类为：“优秀”、“良好”、“中上”、“一般”或“差”。被评为“一般”或“差”的语句随后被排除。

items.en: The second issue pertains to formal statements that, although provable, are based on inconsistent hypotheses leading to vacuous conclusions, rendering the conclusions meaningless in mathematics. For example, consider the following model-generated statement:
items.zh: 第二个问题涉及那些虽然可证明，但基于不一致假设导致空洞结论的形式化语句，使得结论在数学上毫无意义。例如，考虑以下模型生成的语句：

items.en: Here, the hypothesis z2=−1∧z3=−1∧z6=1superscript𝑧21superscript𝑧31superscript𝑧61z^{2}=-1\wedge z^{3}=-1\wedge z^{6}=1 for all complex numbers is clearly false, making any derived conclusions meaningless. To eliminate such cases from our dataset, we implemented a hypothesis rejection method. This involves using the DeepSeek-Prover model to attempt proving the formal statement with ’False’ as the conclusion. A successful proof indicates an invalid hypothesis, prompting exclusion of the statement. An example is shown below:
items.zh: 这里，对于所有复数，假设 z^2 = -1 ∧ z^3 = -1 ∧ z^6 = 1 显然是错误的，使得任何推导出的结论都毫无意义。为了从我们的数据集中消除此类情况，我们实现了一种假设拒绝方法。这涉及使用 DeepSeek-Prover 模型尝试证明以“False”为结论的形式化语句。成功的证明表明假设无效，从而排除该语句。示例如下：

items.en: By applying this dual strategy of model scoring and hypothesis rejection, we curated a refined set of 712,073 high-quality formal statements, providing a robust foundation for further proof synthesis.
items.zh: 通过应用这种模型评分和假设拒绝的双重策略，我们整理了一个包含 712,073 个高质量形式化语句的精炼数据集，为进一步的证明合成提供了坚实基础。

items.en: After creating a substantial corpus of high-quality formal statements, we employed the model to search for proofs of these statements. Traditionally, language models have been used predominantly in a brute-force manner to prove theorems—repeatedly attempting until a valid proof is found or computational resources are exhausted. This approach is inefficient for our purposes. Typically, language models are applied to human-curated formal statements that are carefully crafted and generally true and provable; however, in our task of proving autoformalized statements, many of the statements produced by the model may be incorrect. Indeed, it is unreasonable to expect the model to validate a false proposition within any reliable proof system. This issue becomes more pronounced during large-scale autoformalization, where we observed that at least 20% of the formal statements generated by our model, even after quality filtering, were incorrect, leading to significant computational waste if addressed with brute force.
items.zh: 在创建了大量高质量形式化语句的语料库后，我们使用模型搜索这些语句的证明。传统上，语言模型主要以暴力方式用于证明定理——反复尝试直到找到有效证明或计算资源耗尽。这种方法对我们的目的而言效率低下。通常，语言模型应用于人工整理的形式化语句，这些语句经过精心设计，通常是正确且可证明的；然而，在我们证明自动形式化语句的任务中，模型生成的许多语句可能是不正确的。实际上，期望模型在任何可靠的证明系统中验证一个错误命题是不合理的。在大规模自动形式化过程中，这个问题变得更加突出，我们观察到，即使经过质量过滤，模型生成的形式化语句中至少有 20% 是不正确的，如果采用暴力方式处理，将导致大量的计算浪费。

items.en: To minimize resource wastage on unprovable statements and improve the efficiency of the proof search process, we exploited the logical symmetry between a statement and its negation to accelerate proof synthesis. We implemented dual concurrent proof searches for each synthetic statement—one for the statement Γ⊢PprovesΓ𝑃\Gamma\vdash P and another for its negation Γ⊢¬PprovesΓ𝑃\Gamma\vdash\neg P. The search terminates as soon as a valid proof is found for either, conclusively demonstrating the unprovability of the other. Each proof search stream attempts up to k𝑘k proofs unless a valid proof emerges sooner.
items.zh: 为了最小化在不可证明语句上的资源浪费并提高证明搜索过程的效率，我们利用语句与其否定之间的逻辑对称性来加速证明合成。我们对每个合成语句实施双重并发证明搜索——一个用于语句 Γ ⊢ P，另一个用于其否定 Γ ⊢ ¬P。一旦找到任一的有效证明，搜索即终止，从而明确证明另一方的不可证明性。每个证明搜索流最多尝试 k 次证明，除非提前出现有效证明。

items.en: All validated proofs, whether they justify the original theorems or their negations, are then aggregated to further train the DeepSeek-Prover. Thus, this dual approach serves as a form of data augmentation, enriching the dataset with both propositions and their negations—even if the original propositions were not correctly formalized by the model.
items.zh: 所有经过验证的证明，无论是证明原始定理还是其否定，都会被汇总以进一步训练 DeepSeek-Prover。因此，这种双重方法作为一种数据增强形式，用命题及其否定丰富了数据集——即使原始命题未被模型正确形式化。

items.en: Since the entire pipeline heavily relies on the DeepSeek-Prover, enhancing the model’s performance after each iteration is crucial. To achieve this, we consistently fine-tune the model with newly generated data. The updated model is then utilized for subsequent autoformalization iterations. The key insight from this iterative process is that the model incrementally improves in strength and efficacy after each cycle of refinement and application. This iterative process continues until no further gains are observed. Consequently, the theorem-proof pairs generated by the model become increasingly higher in quality with each iteration. This method ensures that the DeepSeek-Prover consistently enhances its performance, ultimately producing superior theorem-proof pairs through continuous refinement.
items.zh: 由于整个流程严重依赖 DeepSeek-Prover，每次迭代后提升模型性能至关重要。为此，我们持续使用新生成的数据对模型进行微调。更新后的模型随后用于后续的自动形式化迭代。这一迭代过程的关键见解是，模型在每一轮细化和应用后逐步增强其能力和效果。这一迭代过程持续进行，直到不再观察到进一步的提升。因此，模型生成的定理-证明对随着每次迭代质量越来越高。这种方法确保 DeepSeek-Prover 持续提升其性能，最终通过持续细化产生更优的定理-证明对。

items.en: DeepSeek-Prover is built upon DeepSeekMath-Base 7B model (Shao et al., 2024), a decoder-only transformer (Vaswani et al., 2017) pre-trained on a corpus comprising 120 billion math-related tokens. We fine-tuned this model using a global batch size of 512 and a constant learning rate of 1×10−41superscript1041\times 10^{-4}, incorporating 6,000 warmup steps with synthetic data. DeepSeek-Prover’s performance was evaluated against several baselines:
items.zh: DeepSeek-Prover 基于 DeepSeekMath-Base 7B 模型（Shao 等人，2024），这是一个仅解码器的 Transformer（Vaswani 等人，2017），在包含 1200 亿数学相关 token 的语料库上进行了预训练。我们使用全局批次大小 512 和恒定学习率 1×10^{-4} 对该模型进行了微调，并包含 6,000 步预热，使用合成数据。DeepSeek-Prover 的性能与几个基线进行了比较：

items.en: GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 (Achiam et al., 2023), developed by OpenAI, are advanced generative AI models known for their effectiveness in diverse tasks, including code generation. Although not explicitly designed for theorem proving, their extensive scale and parameter count confer significant capabilities. In contrast, DeepSeekMath is a specialized model, explicitly pre-trained for mathematical content. We utilized both GPT-4 (specifically the GPT-4-turbo 0409 version) and DeepSeekMath to generate complete proofs for given theorems using a methodology similar to ours.
items.zh: GPT-3.5 和 GPT-4（Achiam 等人，2023），由 OpenAI 开发，是先进的生成式 AI 模型，以其在包括代码生成在内的多种任务中的有效性而闻名。虽然并非专门为定理证明设计，但其广泛的规模和参数数量赋予了显著的能力。相比之下，DeepSeekMath 是一个专门模型，明确针对数学内容进行了预训练。我们使用了 GPT-4（特别是 GPT-4-turbo 0409 版本）和 DeepSeekMath，使用与我们类似的方法为给定定理生成完整证明。

items.en: GPT-f (Polu and Sutskever, 2020), utilizing a GPT-2-inspired architecture (Radford et al., 2019), implements an iterative best-first search method to progressively generate and validate proof steps within a formal proof setting until a proof is either completed or resources are depleted. This methodology has been further advanced by Proof Artifact Co-Training (Han et al., 2021), ReProver (Yang et al., 2024), Llemma (Azerbayev et al., 2023), and COPRA (Thakur et al., 2023), which employ either specialized fine-tuned models or versatile general-purpose models such as GPT-3.5 and GPT-4 for the generation of proof steps.
items.zh: GPT-f（Polu 和 Sutskever，2020）利用基于 GPT-2 的架构（Radford 等人，2019），实现了一种迭代最佳优先搜索方法，在形式化证明环境中逐步生成和验证证明步骤，直到证明完成或资源耗尽。该方法已通过 Proof Artifact Co-Training（Han 等人，2021）、ReProver（Yang 等人，2024）、Llemma（Azerbayev 等人，2023）和 COPRA（Thakur 等人，2023）进一步发展，这些方法使用专门的微调模型或通用的多用途模型（如 GPT-3.5 和 GPT-4）来生成证明步骤。

items.en: This study addresses complex mathematical problems in algebra and number theory. We evaluate the theorem-proving efficacy of our model using the miniF2F (Zheng et al., 2021) and FIMO (Liu et al., 2023) benchmarks. The metric pass@k is employed to denote the scenario where at least one valid proof is discovered among the first k attempts generated by the model.
items.zh: 本研究解决了代数和数论中的复杂数学问题。我们使用 miniF2F（Zheng 等人，2021）和 FIMO（Liu 等人，2023）基准评估了模型的定理证明效果。采用指标 pass@k 表示在模型生成的前 k 次尝试中至少发现一个有效证明的情况。

items.en: Results on MiniF2F. The miniF2F benchmark consists of 244 validation and 244 test problems, ranging from basic arithmetic to competition-level problems, e.g., problems from the American Invitational Mathematics Examination (AIME), the American Mathematics Competitions (AMC), and the International Mathematical Olympiad (IMO). We use the version of miniF2F in Lean 4, which was released by the LeanDojo project (https://github.com/yangky11/miniF2F-lean4).
items.zh: MiniF2F 上的结果。miniF2F 基准包含 244 个验证问题和 244 个测试问题，范围从基础算术到竞赛级问题，例如来自美国邀请数学考试（AIME）、美国数学竞赛（AMC）和国际数学奥林匹克（IMO）的问题。我们使用 Lean 4 版本的 miniF2F，由 LeanDojo 项目发布（https://github.com/yangky11/miniF2F-lean4）。

items.en: Results on FIMO. The FIMO benchmark comprises 149 formal problems which are sourced from the IMO shortlist translated into Lean 4. Our method successfully proved 4 theorems with 100 attempts per theorem, whereas GPT-4 failed to prove any. By increasing the number of attempts per theorem to 4,096, we successfully proved an additional theorem. Examples of proved theorems of FIMO can be found in Appendix A.3.2.
items.zh: FIMO 上的结果。FIMO 基准包含 149 个形式化问题，这些问题来自 IMO 短名单，并翻译为 Lean 4。我们的方法成功证明了 4 个定理，每个定理尝试 100 次，而 GPT-4 未能证明任何定理。通过将每个定理的尝试次数增加到 4,096 次，我们成功证明了另一个定理。FIMO 已证明定理的示例见附录 A.3.2。

items.en: To demonstrate the effectiveness of large-scale autoformalization, we conducted a comparative analysis as shown in Table 2 between our autoformalized dataset and conventional datasets using expert iteration (Polu and Sutskever, 2020). This iterative method entails generating formal proofs, fine-tune the model based on successful outcomes, and iterating this process until no additional enhancements are observed. The results indicate that models trained with our autoformalized data significantly outperform those trained solely with mathlib data.
items.zh: 为了展示大规模自动形式化的有效性，我们进行了如表 2 所示的比较分析，比较了我们的自动形式化数据集和使用专家迭代（Polu 和 Sutskever，2020）的传统数据集。这种迭代方法包括生成形式化证明，基于成功结果微调模型，并重复此过程直到不再观察到额外改进。结果表明，使用我们的自动形式化数据训练的模型显著优于仅使用 mathlib 数据训练的模型。

items.en: To demonstrate the effectiveness of the model in filtering out low-quality statements, we fine-tuned the DeepSeekMath-Base model using an equal amount of high-score proof data and low-score proof data to verify the quality of the data, as shown in Table 3. The table shows that the model trained on high-score proof data outperformed the model trained on low-score proof data by 4.5%. This enhancement underscores the utility of the model in accurately scoring and effectively filtering out lower-quality statements.
items.zh: 为了展示模型在过滤低质量语句方面的有效性，我们使用等量的高分证明数据和低分证明数据对 DeepSeekMath-Base 模型进行了微调，以验证数据质量，如表 3 所示。该表显示，在高分证明数据上训练的模型比在低分证明数据上训练的模型性能高出 4.5%。这一提升强调了模型在准确评分和有效过滤低质量语句方面的实用性。

items.en: Our investigation into synthetic theorem proving data reveals a clear correlation between dataset size and model efficacy, as illustrated in Table 5. By examining subsets of the eight million generated proof data points, we observed that performance on the miniF2F benchmark improves proportionally to the exponential increase in dataset size. This pattern highlights the pivotal importance of large-scale datasets for boosting model proficiency in automatically formalizing natural language questions. These findings emphasize the significant potential and necessity of systematic data construction for progressing in the field of automated theorem proving.
items.zh: 我们对合成定理证明数据的调查揭示了数据集大小与模型效能之间的明确相关性，如表 5 所示。通过检查八百万生成证明数据点的子集，我们观察到 miniF2F 基准上的性能随数据集大小的指数增长而按比例提升。这一模式突显了大规模数据集对于提升模型自动形式化自然语言问题能力的关键重要性。这些发现强调了系统化数据构建在推进自动定理证明领域的巨大潜力和必要性。

items.en: This section presents two case studies to demonstrate the application of our methods in autoformalizing theorems. It showcases both successful proofs and the identification of inconsistencies during the Hypothesis Rejection stage.
items.zh: 本节通过两个案例研究展示我们的方法在自动形式化定理中的应用。它展示了成功证明以及在假设拒绝阶段识别不一致性的情况。

full.secZh: 5.1 带完整证明的自动形式化定理

items.en: Example a. Problem: Prove that the determinant of the following matrix is zero.
items.zh: 示例 a. 问题：证明以下矩阵的行列式为零。

items.en: This approach effectively translates the algebraic expression of the matrix and its determinant into a formal language using Lean. The autoformalization captures the essence of the original mathematical statement by defining a specific 3×3333\times 3 matrix dependent on real numbers a𝑎a and b𝑏b, and asserts that its determinant is zero. The formalization employs the Matrix.det function to compute the determinant, utilizing the ![...] notation for lists of lists in Lean to represent the matrix rows.
items.zh: 这种方法使用 Lean 有效地将矩阵及其行列式的代数表达式转换为形式化语言。自动形式化通过定义一个依赖于实数 a 和 b 的特定 3×3 矩阵，并断言其行列式为零，捕捉了原始数学陈述的本质。形式化使用 Matrix.det 函数计算行列式，利用 Lean 中的 ![...] 列表的列表表示法来表示矩阵的行。

full.sec: 5.2 Autoformalization of Theorem with Inconsistent Hypotheses
full.secZh: 5.2 假设不一致的定理自动形式化

items.en: Example b. Problem: Given a real number D𝐷D and the condition that for non-zero real numbers a,b,c𝑎𝑏𝑐a,b,c, the determinant of the matrix [abc149312]matrix𝑎𝑏𝑐149312\begin{bmatrix}a&b&c\\ 1&4&9\\ 3&1&2\end{bmatrix} equals D𝐷D, prove that D2=154superscript𝐷2154D^{2}=154.
items.zh: 示例 b. 问题：给定实数 D，且对于非零实数 a, b, c，矩阵 [a b c; 1 4 9; 3 1 2] 的行列式等于 D，证明 D² = 154。

items.en: The initial autoformalization incorrectly assumes that the condition D2=154superscript𝐷2154D^{2}=154 universally applies to all non-zero real numbers a𝑎a, b𝑏b, and c𝑐c. This assumption is not supported by the problem statement, which does not claim universal applicability. Instead, the formalization should aim to either identify specific values of a𝑎a, b𝑏b, and c𝑐c that satisfy D2=154superscript𝐷2154D^{2}=154 or demonstrate that no such values exist.
items.zh: 初始的自动形式化错误地假设条件 D² = 154 普遍适用于所有非零实数 a、b 和 c。这一假设并未得到问题陈述的支持，该陈述并未声称普遍适用。相反，形式化应旨在要么找出满足 D² = 154 的特定 a、b、c 值，要么证明不存在这样的值。

items.en: The model successfully identifies this inconsistency and provides a counterexample to demonstrate the absurdity of the hypothesis:
items.zh: 模型成功识别了这一不一致性，并提供了一个反例来证明假设的荒谬性：

items.en: A corrected version of the autoformalized theorem can be proposed as follows:
items.zh: 可以提出自动形式化定理的修正版本如下：

items.en: These examples illustrate the model’s capability to verify proofs and identify hypothesis inconsistencies effectively. Further details can be found in Appendix A.2.
items.zh: 这些示例说明了模型有效验证证明和识别假设不一致性的能力。更多细节见附录 A.2。

items.en: In this paper, we presented a method to generate extensive synthetic proof data from high-school and undergraduate-level mathematical competition problems. By translating natural language problems into formal statements, filtering out low-quality ones, and using iterative proof generation, we created 8 million proof data points and significantly improved the DeepSeekMath 7B model’s performance in ATP when trained on this synthetic data. Our model outperforms GPT-4 and other methods on benchmarks like miniF2F and FIMO. By open-sourcing our dataset and model, we aim to advance research in automated theorem proving and enhance the capabilities of large language models in formal mathematical reasoning. Currently, our work mainly focuses on algebra and number theory at the middle school and undergraduate levels. In future work, we will aim to expand the diversity of mathematical problems addressed, enhancing the general applicability of our methods in ATP.
items.zh: 在本文中，我们提出了一种方法，从高中和本科水平的数学竞赛问题中生成大量合成证明数据。通过将自然语言问题翻译成形式化陈述，过滤低质量问题，并使用迭代证明生成，我们创建了 800 万个证明数据点，并在使用这些合成数据训练时显著提高了 DeepSeekMath 7B 模型在自动定理证明中的性能。我们的模型在 miniF2F 和 FIMO 等基准测试上优于 GPT-4 和其他方法。通过开源我们的数据集和模型，我们旨在推进自动定理证明的研究，并增强大型语言模型在形式化数学推理中的能力。目前，我们的工作主要集中于初中和本科水平的代数和数论。在未来的工作中，我们将致力于扩展所处理数学问题的多样性，增强我们的方法在自动定理证明中的普遍适用性。

items.en: The research presented in this paper has the potential to significantly advance automated theorem proving by leveraging large-scale synthetic proof data generated from informal mathematical problems. This remarkable advancement can enhance the capabilities of large language models in formal theorem proving, contributing to more reliable mathematical proof verification and providing valuable educational resources for students and researchers. By directly releasing the code, model, and data, we aim to ensure the responsible use of our work, fostering further innovation and maintaining high standards of data privacy and intellectual property compliance.
items.zh: 本文提出的研究通过利用从非正式数学问题生成的大规模合成证明数据，有潜力显著推进自动定理证明。这一显著进步可以增强大型语言模型在形式化定理证明中的能力，有助于更可靠的数学证明验证，并为学生和研究人员提供宝贵的教育资源。通过直接发布代码、模型和数据，我们旨在确保我们工作的负责任使用，促进进一步创新，并维护数据隐私和知识产权合规的高标准。
